articles.title: New Zealand Shares Finish Lower
articles.expand: The NZX 50 fell 86 points, or 0.7%, to close at 12,826 on Wednesday, giving up earlier gains after advancing in the previous session. Investors stayed cautious ahead of next week’s Reserve Bank of New Zealand policy meeting, as the ongoing war continued to cloud the global outlook. Sentiment was further dampened by weaker Chinese factory activity, ...

articles.expand: The dollar is winning the safe-haven battle — for now. But with an ECB rate hike increasingly priced in and an Iran deadline days away, EUR/USD may not stay quiet for long.

articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) trades slightly higher against its major currency peers, rising 0.12% to near 1.3242, during the Asian trading session on Wednesday.

articles.title: Is risk-on, back on? The AUDUSD is higher but it is not running quickly to the upside.
articles.expand: The AUDUSD typically trades as a classic risk-on/risk-off barometer. When equities are under pressure and yields are pushing higher, the pair tends to drift lower as traders reduce risk exposure. Conversely, when stocks firm and yields ease, AUDUSD often benefits from a more constructive, risk-on backdrop. Today, the pair is higher by about 0.62%, ...

articles.title: Indian rupee hits fresh record low
articles.expand: India’s rupee fell to a record low of more than 95 to the dollar on Monday, before recovering, despite recent efforts by the central bank to stem its fall.

articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) is showing a technical recovery, breaking $4,600/oz as escalating Middle East tensions and a shifting correlation with rising Oil prices boost the "inflation hedge" narrative. Will the bullish momentum continue?

The pair has since rotated back toward unchanged on the day, currently trading near 1.3180 after reaching a high of 1.3264...

articles.expand: The USDJPY has rotated lower after failing above the 160.00 level, a key psychological and technical barrier. Recall, the pair broke above that level on Friday, reaching a high of 160.455, but the breakout lacked follow-through. articles.title: EURUSD moves higher to test 200 hour MA. Above the 100 hour MA.
articles.expand: The EURUSD is seeing a sharp bounce higher, helped by a more supportive macro backdrop. Stocks are moving higher, yields are lower on the day, and oil has come off its highs, all of which are weighing on the USD and giving the pair a lift. articles.expand: Canada’s economy expanded by 0.1% mom in January, beating expectations of a flat reading, as strength in resource-related sectors helped offset ongoing weakness in manufacturing. articles.title: Eurozone headline inflation picks up in March as energy prices surge on Middle East war
articles.expand: The preliminary CPI report for March sees headline annual inflation come in at 2.5%, just a touch softer than the 2.6% estimate. Still, it represents a notable bump to the 1.9% reading in February last month. The main cause for the jump is higher energy prices, as a result of the US-Iran conflict. Of note, energy prices were seen 4.9% higher than i...

articles.title: Eurozone CPI Jumps to 2.5% as Energy Drives Rebound, Core Inflation Eases Slightly
articles.expand: Eurozone inflation accelerated sharply in March, with headline CPI rising from 1.9% yoy to 2.5% yoy, matching expectations and marking the highest level since January 2025. The jump was driven primarily by a reversal in energy prices, which surged from -3.1% yoy to 4.9% yoy, reflecting the impact of rising oil costs linked to ongoing […] The ...

articles.expand: The BOJ’s March summary shows a board in crisis: hike rates to defend the yen, or stay low and watch it burn?
